userIntent: Review a PR that adds streaming responses and backpressure handling.

state.title: Streaming/backpressure PR review
state.summary: The PR moves response generation to streaming and adds a queue-based backpressure layer, but the cancellation path and high-water behavior need fixes before merge.

motivation.before: Responses were buffered fully before being sent, increasing latency and memory use.
motivation.after: Chunks stream as they arrive, with queue backpressure intended to protect memory.

files.role: Creates stream controller and chunk writer
notes: Backpressure signal is observed but not propagated to producer.
Drop behavior is unclear for user-visible chunks.

findings.title: Abort signal does not stop the producer
findings.impact: Client disconnects can keep upstream work running and consume memory.
findings.suggestion: Wire the request AbortSignal through the producer loop and close the queue on abort.
findings.codeExcerpt: + for await (const chunk of producer()) { await queue.push(chunk) }

findings.title: High-water policy can reorder terminal chunks
findings.impact: A final error or done marker may be dropped behind data chunks, leaving clients hanging.
findings.suggestion: Make terminal events non-droppable and drain or close explicitly.

findings.title: Tests do not cover slow consumers
findings.impact: Regression risk remains around delayed readers.
findings.suggestion: Add a fake slow writable and assert bounded queue length.
